sourceSwitcher.label: Doc source
sourceSwitcher.description: Switch between shared team docs and your private docs.

dialog.title: Create a structured doc
dialog.description: Choose the collaboration shape this doc should carry. Synaply will insert a starter structure for the team.
dialog.projectHint: This doc will be linked to the current project automatically.
dialog.parentHint: This doc will be created inside the current folder.

blank.label: Blank doc
blank.description: Start from an empty page when you just need a free-form note.
blank.title: New document

projectBrief.label: Project brief
projectBrief.description: Clarify why this project exists, what is in scope, and what success looks like.
projectBrief.title: Project brief

decisionLog.label: Decision log
decisionLog.description: Capture the decision, rationale, impacted work, and follow-up so handoffs stay grounded.
decisionLog.title: Decision log

reviewPacket.label: Review packet
reviewPacket.description: Package the context, changes, and questions reviewers need before giving a response.
reviewPacket.title: Review packet

handoffPacket.label: Handoff packet
handoffPacket.description: Prepare the next owner with readiness notes, dependencies, links, and expectations.
handoffPacket.title: Handoff packet

releaseChecklist.label: Release checklist
releaseChecklist.description: Keep launch readiness, risk checks, owners, and post-release watchpoints in one place.
releaseChecklist.title: Release checklist

projectBrief.intro: Summarize what this project is trying to move forward and why the team should align around it now.
projectBrief.goal: [Goal and scope] Describe the delivery goal, scope boundary, and what this project will intentionally not cover.
projectBrief.scope: [Key context] Capture the current context, constraints, dependencies, and any assumptions the team should hold.
projectBrief.success: [Success signal] Explain how the team will know this project is ready, successful, or safe to ship.
projectBrief.openQuestions: [Open questions] List the unresolved questions, decisions, or risks that still need follow-up.

decisionLog.intro: Use this note to preserve one concrete decision so the next owner can understand the why without replaying the entire discussion.
decisionLog.decision: [Decision] State the decision in one sentence.
decisionLog.context: [Context] Explain what changed, what pressure triggered the decision, or what tradeoff needed resolution.
decisionLog.rationale: [Rationale] Write why this option won over the alternatives.
decisionLog.impact: [Affected work] Link the impacted project, issue, workflow, or release surface and note what changes next.
decisionLog.followUp: [Follow-up] Capture the next actions, owners, and the point when this decision should be revisited.

reviewPacket.intro: Package the minimum context a reviewer needs so the review can move asynchronously without extra chasing.
reviewPacket.goal: [Review goal] Clarify what kind of review you need and what decision should come out of it.
reviewPacket.changeSummary: [What changed] Summarize the proposal, implementation, or artifact under review.
reviewPacket.focusQuestions: [Reviewer focus] List the questions, risks, or edge cases you want reviewers to inspect closely.
reviewPacket.decision: [Decision / requested changes] Leave space to record the outcome, requested changes, or approval notes.
reviewPacket.nextStep: [Next handoff] Explain what should happen after review and who is expected to take over.

handoffPacket.intro: Use this packet to make the next owner productive without needing another synchronous walkthrough.
handoffPacket.target: [Handoff target] State who this handoff is for and what they are expected to take over.
handoffPacket.readyNow: [Ready now] Summarize what is already done, verified, and safe for the next owner to trust.
handoffPacket.dependencies: [Dependencies and blockers] List open blockers, risks, decisions, or inputs still needed.
handoffPacket.links: [Links and evidence] Attach the issue, doc, design, PR, or release context the receiver should read first.
handoffPacket.receiverChecklist: [Receiver checklist] Spell out the first checks or actions the receiver should complete after accepting the handoff.

releaseChecklist.intro: Keep launch readiness visible so product, design, engineering, ops, and support can self-serve the current state.
releaseChecklist.scope: [Release scope] Describe what is shipping, what is intentionally excluded, and what environments are affected.
releaseChecklist.risk: [Risks and safeguards] Record the visible risks, rollback path, monitoring, and any coordination dependencies.
releaseChecklist.goNoGo: [Go / no-go checks] List the checks that must pass before release is considered ready.
releaseChecklist.ownerMap: [Owner map] Make clear who owns approval, execution, communication, and post-release follow-up.
releaseChecklist.postRelease: [Post-release watch] Capture what the team should monitor immediately after release and what thresholds require action.

consumption.summaryEmpty: No structured doc has been linked yet. Start with the packet the next owner will need most.
consumption.emptyDescription: This collaboration packet has not been captured yet.

deleteDialog.title: Delete document?
deleteDialog.description: This will delete “{title}”. {children} This action cannot be undone.
deleteDialog.children: Its child documents will be deleted too.

blocknote.conflict: A remote change was detected. Your local draft has been preserved.
blocknote.conflictStatus: Conflict detected. Your local draft has been preserved.
blocknote.errorStatus: Save failed. Your local draft has been preserved locally.
blocknote.shortcut: Press {key} + S to save • Auto-saves every 30 seconds

folderIntro.saveFailed: Failed to save the folder description.
folderIntro.title: Folder description
folderIntro.emptyEditable: Click to add a folder description...

folderIntro.tip2: Right-click the folder to create child documents or child folders.
folderIntro.tip3: You can add detailed guidance and usage notes here.
